zh.lifeSummary: 當Amy Smith於1521年出生在英國英格蘭艾可利文哈爾時，她的父親Johannes Smythe, Lord of Blackmoor Manor21歲，母親Agnes Idle20歲。她於1539年與John Amptyll在英國倫敦結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1607年在英國英格蘭米德萊可易亞斯特 倫敦享年87歲，埋葬在英國倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Amy Smith于1521年出生在英国英格兰艾可利文哈爾时，她的父亲Johannes Smythe, Lord of Blackmoor Manor21岁，母亲Agnes Idle20岁。她于1539年与John Amptyll在英国倫敦结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1607年在英国英格兰米德萊可易亞斯特 倫敦享年87岁，埋葬在英国倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smithe
meanings: English: variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: Smythe
meanings: English (London): variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: English:
occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Amy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimie
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimée
meanings: originally a vernacular nickname meaning ‘beloved’, from the past participle of French aimer ‘to love’ (Latin amare; compare Amy ). It has been in use in French since the Middle Ages, although it has never been very common. It is now also used, with or without the accent, as a given name in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Amie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Amy  , corresponding in form to the French vocabulary word meaning ‘(female) friend’.

variants.name: Ami
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Amy Smith was born in 1521, in Rivenhall, Essex, England, United Kingdom, her father, Johannes Smythe, Lord of Blackmoor Manor, was 21 and her mother, Agnes Idle, was 20. She married John Amptyll in 1539, in London, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died about 1607, in East London, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 87, and was buried in London, England.

fr.St Giles in the Fields, Middlesex, Engla: St Giles in the Fields, Middlesex, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Amy Smith est née en 1521, à Rivenhall, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Johannes Smythe, Lord of Blackmoor Manor, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Agnes Idle, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé John Amptyll en 1539, à Londres, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1607, à East London, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 87 ans, et a été enterrée à Londres, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Amy Smith(이)가 1521년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 라이븐홀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Johannes Smythe, Lord of Blackmoor Manor(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Idle(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1539년 영국 런던에서 John Amptyll(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1607년 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 이스트 런던에서 87세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 런던에 묻혔다.

ru.St Giles in the Fields, Middlesex, Engla: ст джилесь ин т фиелдс, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Amy Smith родилась в 1521 г. в местности ривенолл, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Johannes Smythe, Lord of Blackmoor Manor было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Agnes Idle, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Amptyll в 1539 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1607 г. в местности эаст лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 87 лет и была похоронена в местности Лондон, Англия.

es.St Giles in the Fields, Middlesex, Engla: St Giles in the Fields, Middlesex, Inglaterra
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Amy Smith nació en 1521, en Rivenhall, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Johannes Smythe, Lord of Blackmoor Manor, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Agnes Idle, tenía 20 años. Se casó con John Amptyll en 1539, en London, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1607, en East London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 87 años, y fue sepultada en London, Inglaterra.
